subject: Re:
body:
take photos

--- Gmax <gmax1@mindspring.com> wrote:

> Have arrived safely in Bhutan  - landing here makes
> Cusco look like JFK on a clear beautiful day on a
> straight in! It was very very hairy. There were 9 of
> us on the plane and when we landed everyone 
> clapped! The high point of the pre landing is that
> we flew over Everest - the low point followed when
> we then dive bombed the runway, zigzagging through
> the mountain pass- it was amazing to see and to
> feel. 
> Bhutan is simply breath taking - it is completley
> and utterly unspoilt, untouched by time or the
> outside world. This is not a country you should
> miss. I am having problems w/the altitude though - i
> feel quite dizzy and am having a hard time
> breathing, but am sure I will feel better tom. I
> have already had tea w/a lama  - an altitude
> recuperative tea! A monk, not a hairy 4 legged
> animal in case you were wondering. 
> The Sat phone works here - BB and regular phone no.
> Obviously I have internet though - in my bedroom no
> less which makes me happy.
> Will e mail you periodic updates
> Gx-----Original Message-----
> From: "J. Epstein" <jeeproject@yahoo.com>

body_html:
take photos

--- Gmax gmax1@mindspring.com wrote:

Have arrived safely in Bhutan - landing here makes
Cusco look like JFK on a clear beautiful day on a
straight in! It was very very hairy. There were 9 of
us on the plane and when we landed everyone
clapped! The high point of the pre landing is that
we flew over Everest - the low point followed when
we then dive bombed the runway, zigzagging through
the mountain pass- it was amazing to see and to
feel.
Bhutan is simply breath taking - it is completley
and utterly unspoilt, untouched by time or the
outside world. This is not a country you should
miss. I am having problems w/the altitude though - i
feel quite dizzy and am having a hard time
breathing, but am sure I will feel better tom. I
have already had tea w/a lama - an altitude
recuperative tea! A monk, not a hairy 4 legged
animal in case you were wondering.
The Sat phone works here - BB and regular phone no.
Obviously I have internet though - in my bedroom no
less which makes me happy.
Will e mail you periodic updates
Gx-----Original Message-----
From: "J. Epstein" jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Oct 13, 2005 7:11 AM
To: gmax1@mindspring.com
Subject: Re:

have fun
